pages.full_text:
Friday May 7th.

6.25t we left Westfield [?S] & drove to Moreton on the Marsh, Simon had forgotten the map so we didn't find out original campsite near Blockley. We ended up arriving too late (8.10) & slept in carpark [illegible].

__Saturday May 8__th

Cold, miserable weather. By 8.30 we were having breakfast at William's & Bryony's B&B in Lower Swell, Cotswolds. Very fancy B&B, so posh Simon had to repark the van. Old vine in the conservatory.
By 10 we were at __Pangolin Foundry__ in Chalford. Andy, the designer had organised for a group of students to visit & find out about mould-making & casting metal sculptures - hand sized giants (Bull in Bull ring). Alloys - bronze. (Cornley's are cast in iron, industrially, so he doesn't use them) lots of work for [?Admira] [?Hirst]. 92 people work there. Very interesting. Lost wax. Sand moulds [?or] with resin, injected with CO₂. Sue, the designer, asked lots of questions.
     We had lunch in the van, then rested. At 4.40, W&B picked us up in their super car & drove us to Winchcombe to David Thistlethwaite's
pages.notes: First word after 'Westfield' is unclear — possibly an initial or abbreviation. 'Admira Hirst' may be 'Damien Hirst' misread. 'Cornley's' spelling uncertain. No handwritten page number visible. Year not determinable from this page alone — volume B covers 2003–2019; 'May 7th Friday' fell on e.g. 2004, 2010, 2021 — within volume range 2010 is plausible but unconfirmed. 'carpark' word at end of first entry partially obscured.

pages.full_text:
& Sarah Loveday's exhibition of paintings. It was good to see David & Ali after so many years.
     We went out to supper at the Fox, lovely walnut & honey tart. Then back to our campsite, __Folly Farm__, **** £12 where we're parked under beautiful apple blossom.

__Sunday May 9__th

     Slightly better weather today. We again breakfasted with William & Bryony. The landlady's husband had been an architect for townhalls. We think he bought the old rectory farm & then found the foundations of out buildings & managed to build about 3 more Cotswold houses in the [?old] garden: Glebe House, Barn Cottage etc.
     We set off to Snowshill Manor, having a bit of trouble with a cycle race enroute. The garden to Snowshill Manor is the most lovely I've seen, on the side of a hill, compartments. Benches, ponds, barns. Model village, old fire engines. Charles Wade's collection of 22,000 objects grew so large that he stored it in [?&] the Manor house & moved, with his wife, into the priest's
pages.notes: Text continues onto next page — entry for Sunday May 9th is cut off mid-sentence. '**** £12' appears to be a campsite rating notation next to Folly Farm. 'out buildings' may be 'outbuildings' written as two words. Year not determinable from this page alone.
